ccel.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second
ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.text: Let your Mercury be depicted with a Saraballum, and with wings on his helmet or his cap, and in other respects naked.
I see a marvellous thing, a god flying with a little satchel.
Run, poor creatures, with your lap spread open when he flies, that he may empty his satchel: do ye from thence be prepared.
Look on the painted one, since he will thus cast you money from on high: then dance ye securely.
Vain man, art thou not mad, to worship painted gods in heaven?
If thou knowest not how to live, continue to dwell with the beasts.
